chapter_outlines.title: Shards of Authority
chapter_outlines.chapter_goal: Force the ten agents to confront the cost of unifying narrative—an act that may silence dissenting voices or fracture the world into endless shards, raising stakes through an echo‑phage assault.
arc_targets: Reveal the Echoed Gaze’s true binding mechanism (glass lattice) and its catalytic echo‑phage release
Confronting the paradox of a single voice in a world of echoes
Balancing unity with diversity of intent
next_arc_targets: Elisa wrestles with the loss of her voice and the looming shadow of her mother's absence.
The lattice’s collapse creates a fissure that begins to suck in surrounding memories.
Establish a tangible consequence for binding—one fragment is irrevocably erased
The fragment lost to the void.
Elisa’s secret role in the orchard fire.
The fragment that slipped into the void remains lost; its content and impact on the agents’ future decisions remain unknown.
unresolved_threads: The fragment that slipped into the void remains lost; its content and impact on the agents’ future decisions remain unknown.
planned_payoffs: The Echoed Gaze’s true mechanism is revealed: a lattice that both binds and devours.
One agent’s fragment is irrevocably erased, showing the cost of unity.
A fragment slips into the void, leaving an unresolved wound.
outline_notes: Elisa’s secret burn of orchard fire foreshadows her willingness to sacrifice; her hand on the lattice should feel cold, resigned. The echo‑phage manifests as a shimmering distortion that speaks in fragmented memories, a living shadow of grief. The lattice’s collapse produces a wound in the ground that swallows light - a visual promise of void absorption. The unresolved thread: a fragment falls into darkness beyond the lattice, never to be recovered, planting seed for next chapter.
Confirmed that the Echoed Gaze can bind multiple narrative intents but releases echo‑phages.
Established that binding a fragment locks it but erases it if the lattice collapses.
Added the void rule: spoken fragments can be absorbed if the lattice is intact.
The fragment lost to the void.
Elisa’s secret role in the orchard fire.

Develop character voices and internal conflicts, especially Elisa’s grief and secret.
Embed world rules (moonlit glass, echo‑phage, void absorption) throughout the text.
Maintain lyrical, metaphor‑rich language and appropriate dialogue density.
